claim_to_validate: e-CVT MGU can reduce rear drivetrain exposure and unsprung mass without harming rider control
why_it_matters: This is the core eMTB system value: derailleur/cassette/chain exposure is a real pain, and Avinox Tech Details now makes reduced unsprung mass, torque smoothing and virtual gears a production-direction / quasi-production benchmark.
primary_method: prototype ride + user interview + durability test
comparison_baseline: SRAM/Shimano electronic derailleur + high-performance mid-drive; Pinion geared MGU
minimum_evidence_needed: Prototype eMTB with manual override/virtual gear modes; rider feedback under technical climbing; post-impact and mud/wash reliability checks; rear unsprung mass comparison against external drivetrain
next_action: Add Avinox Tech Details targets: >1.1 kg unsprung mass reduction, torque smoothing, manual/virtual gear control and rear drivetrain simplification.

claim_to_validate: Center-mounted e-CVT architecture offers a real rear-wheel / suspension advantage over hub CVT/internal gear
why_it_matters: If rear wheel mass and suspension response are not materially improved, hub/internal routes may be enough; Avinox claims >1.1 kg reduced unsprung mass, making this a direct benchmark.
comparison_baseline: Hub CVT/internal gear + mid-drive; geared MGU; ordinary mid-drive
minimum_evidence_needed: Rear wheel mass comparison, wheel-service steps, suspension packaging review and ride feedback from aggressive eMTB use
next_action: Quantify whether a center MGU can actually remove >1.1 kg from rear unsprung mass versus modern derailleur/cassette setups.

claim_to_validate: e-CVT efficiency, NVH, torque smoothing and thermal behavior are acceptable in technical climbing and high-torque bursts
why_it_matters: eMTB magnifies efficiency loss, noise and thermal weakness; Avinox claims 45 dB, fast response with no overshoot, 300 Nm load shift and constant torque output.
comparison_baseline: Electronic derailleur mid-drive; geared MGU; ordinary mid-drive
minimum_evidence_needed: Efficiency map at low speed/high torque, repeated climb thermal trace, SPL/noise character, torque response trace and rider perception
next_action: Add torque-response and shifting-window measurement to low-speed high-torque climbing and burst/recovery cycles.

claim_to_validate: e-CVT MGU improves loaded start, slope start, reverse maneuvering and frequent stop-start usability for low-skill users
why_it_matters: This is the main cargo/family UX value and must beat hub CVT/internal gear alternatives; Avinox adds stationary shifting, 300 Nm load shift and electric reverse assist as production-direction / quasi-production targets.
primary_method: prototype ride + family/user test + bench start test
comparison_baseline: enviolo Automatic/Rohloff/3X3 + mid-drive; ordinary mid-drive + mechanical transmission
minimum_evidence_needed: Loaded start success rate, cadence stability, rider error reduction, reverse maneuvering usability, perceived confidence and drivetrain shock comparison
next_action: Define loaded-start, slope start, reverse assist and lock/unlock tests at target gross vehicle weights and interview cargo/family OEMs.

claim_to_validate: e-CVT MGU can deliver lower maintenance or TCO than hub CVT/internal gear and external drivetrain routes
why_it_matters: Cargo/family selection depends on service cost, not only nice riding feel; Avinox claims maintenance-free, 3 times more durable and traditional drivetrain 10% less efficient.
primary_method: TCO model + OEM/dealer interview + service simulation
comparison_baseline: enviolo Automatic; Rohloff E-14; 3X3; electronic derailleur; ordinary drivetrain
minimum_evidence_needed: Maintenance interval assumptions, likely replacement modules, dealer labor time, warranty risk, fleet downtime comparison and test basis for durability/efficiency claims
next_action: Build TCO template and define evidence needed to support or refute 3x durability and 10% efficiency claims.

claim_to_validate: High-load heat and durability are acceptable under long slope, stop-start and daily overload
why_it_matters: Cargo/family can break an e-CVT MGU if thermal and durability margins are weak.
comparison_baseline: Hub CVT/internal gear; geared MGU; ordinary drivetrain
minimum_evidence_needed: Thermal derating profile, lubricant/seal/actuator wear, repeated loaded starts and fault data
next_action: Create high-load thermal cycle based on loaded cargo use and request supplier data if possible.

claim_to_validate: Target cadence, torque smoothing and continuous ratio feel create premium value beyond automatic electronic shifting
why_it_matters: This segment may understand comfort and premium smoothness, but substitute pressure is strong; Avinox Tech Details adds torque smoothing, 45 dB and 100%-520% configurable profiles as product targets.
comparison_baseline: Bosch eShift/Shimano AUTO SHIFT/SRAM electronic shifting; enviolo Automatic; Pinion MGU
minimum_evidence_needed: Blind or structured ride feedback, cadence stability metrics, torque smoothness, perceived noise and willingness-to-pay signal
next_action: Design ride questionnaire comparing target cadence, torque smoothing, stepped auto shift and manual/virtual gear modes.

claim_to_validate: Mid-mounted e-CVT provides meaningful rear-wheel simplification, sensor integration and service benefit over hub CVT/internal gear
why_it_matters: If rear-wheel service is not improved enough, hub CVT/Automatic may be the simpler choice; Avinox adds built-in speed sensor and single-speed drivetrain claims.
minimum_evidence_needed: Wheel removal/service steps, rear-wheel mass, belt/chain path, speed-sensor integration, diagnostic boundary and dealer acceptance
next_action: Map service workflows for hub CVT/internal gear vs e-CVT MGU concept and include built-in speed sensor implications.

claim_to_validate: Efficiency, NVH and service strategy are acceptable for premium long-distance use
why_it_matters: Premium users may reject noisy or inefficient systems despite low-maintenance promise.
primary_method: bench test + long ride + service review
comparison_baseline: enviolo Automatic; Pinion MGU; ordinary mid-drive + mechanical transmission
minimum_evidence_needed: Cruise/climb efficiency map, noise character, long-ride thermal stability, oil/seal/service plan and fault recovery
next_action: Define mixed route efficiency/NVH profile and list service module assumptions.

claim_to_validate: Avinox MG Concept Tech Details are treated as production-direction / quasi-production product-definition targets while mechanism and final public datasheet remain open
why_it_matters: Underweighting Avinox would distort internal urgency; overclaiming mechanism or final certification would distort engineering risk.
primary_method: official source tracking + patent / booth material review
comparison_baseline: Gobao official e-CVT evidence; Pinion official geared MGU evidence
minimum_evidence_needed: Official Avinox technical release, patent evidence, press kit, OEM prototype pages, display OCR, mechanism confirmation and production scope
next_action: Use Tech Details as benchmark targets now; continue tracking press kit, patents and OEM pages for mechanism/test-basis closure.

claim_to_validate: Gobao X-system production readiness, OEM customers and service model are clear enough for benchmark use
why_it_matters: Gobao is the strongest official e-CVT MGU evidence, but production maturity is still uncertain.
comparison_baseline: Pinion MGU production references; Avinox Tech Details production-direction / quasi-production benchmark; Owuru prototype signal
minimum_evidence_needed: Official product naming, production date, OEM customers, service documentation and sustained performance data
next_action: Search official Gobao materials and OEM bike pages for X-system production proof; compare against Avinox Tech Details target set.

claim_to_validate: Failure degradation and service modularity can be made acceptable
why_it_matters: MGU centralizes failures; a good ride concept can still fail if service risk is too high.
comparison_baseline: electronic derailleur service; hub internal gear service; Pinion MGU service model
minimum_evidence_needed: Fault tree, ride-home/push mode, diagnostic codes, module replacement boundaries, dealer training and warranty cost assumptions
next_action: Create service/fault-degradation checklist for supplier or internal architecture review.
